items.title: 'Caught lying': Police bashed a man, then charged him with assaulting them

children.content: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.

gallery.altText: A man winces in agony in the corner of a police holding cell as three officers stand over him.
A man wearing a white polo with blue stripes is pushed into a holding cell by a police officer.

thumbnail.altText: A man winces in agony in the corner of a police holding cell as three officers stand over him.
A man wearing a white polo with blue stripes is pushed into a holding cell by a police officer.

items.title: Joyce defends Hanson's attendance record, says she has 'ability' to be PM

children.content: Barnaby Joyce has defended his leader's parliamentary attendance record after Pauline Hanson came under fire for not showing up to Senate estimates last week. Follow live.

image.altText: Barnaby Joyce and Pauline Hanson

items.title: One Nation overtakes Labor for first time as young Australians reject budget

children.content: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they would rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.

image.altText: A woman with short red hair and a blue blazer with a brooch

items.title: Why is Labor spending its political majority on this tax reform?

children.content: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a large majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?

image.altText: Jim Chalmers and Anthony Albanese stand next to each other; both wear dark suit jackets.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Sydney and Melbourne property values drop as housing market stalls in May

children.content: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.

image.altText: Houses in Brisbane street

items.title: Conviction of Aussie band's 'sixth member' reveals Byron Bay's dark side as women speak out

children.content: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to join dozens of others speaking out about the experiences of young girls in Byron Bay in the early 2000s, where they say it became normalised for older boys and men to groom and have sex with girls then publicly humiliate them.

gallery.altText: Woman with black curly hard and yellow glasses looking out a window frame

thumbnail.altText: Woman with black curly hard and yellow glasses looking out a window frame

items.title: Australia could still buy five US submarines if needed, defence minister says

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: Quietly published Liberal Party paper raises gender membership concerns

children.content: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: Supreme leader hard to find as Trump wants peace plan rewrites

children.content: Donald Trump is reported to be amending a peace plan, which could fuel further delays in finding a final agreement.

articles.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize castle

children.content: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

image.altText: Three soldiers stand looking at an old crumbling structure in the near distance

gallery.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

thumbnail.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

items.title: Three-year-old child dies in Melbourne house fire

children.content: A child has died in a house fire in Werribee and another person has been taken to hospital with serious injuries.

image.altText: A child holds a hose while looking at the camera

items.title: Shein, Kmart fight back against Qld retailer in intellectual property dispute

children.content: Shein and Kmart have fired back at Sabo Skirt in an intellectual property dispute, saying the designs have been accused of copying are substantially similar to pre-existing designs.

image.altText: A woman in a blue dress with a background of other similar shaped dresses.

items.title: WA storm cuts power to thousands as wild weekend weather continues

children.content: Western Australia's southern half endures a second storm in as many days, with the State Emergency Service receiving nearly 700 requests for help across the WA Day long weekend.

gallery.altText: A flooded boat ramp in a high tide during stormy weather.

thumbnail.altText: A flooded boat ramp in a high tide during stormy weather.

items.title: Inside the new 'ghost proxy' terror group 'we need to take seriously'

children.content: Australia would be the "perfect" target for a fledgling Islamist terror network that has carried out antisemitic attacks in Europe, the UK and Canada, experts have warned.

image.altText: Two charred vehicles

items.title: ASX tipped lower despite Wall St gains, AUD flat, oil higher

children.content: ASX futures are pointing to a slight decline to begin winter, with the Australian share market expected to open about 13 points lower. The weakness comes despite a solid finish on Wall Street on Friday, with the Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq all ending higher. Follow our live business blog.

image.altText: Pedestrians are seen reflected on the Australian Stock Exchange trading board in Sydney

items.title: One-third of Ian Thorpe's life was in the spotlight, and it took its toll

children.content: Ian Thorpe became a global star as a teenager, attracting both opportunities and scrutiny. Now, he wishes he'd done some things differently.

gallery.altText: Mid-scream, Ian Thorpe triumphantly raises his fist in the air as he stand at the end of a pool.

items.title: When does getting a 'prenup' or a binding financial agreement make sense?

children.content: Binding financial agreements take time and money to obtain. Here's how they work.

items.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone

children.content: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce, including baked goods and bouquets, and business is booming.

image.altText: A woman with stands next to a wooden stall stocked with loaves of bread. She wears jeans and a tshirt, smiling

items.title: Cattle company’s own tip-off revealed biosecurity breach, court told

children.content: A NSW company tipped off authorities to the illegal movement of its cattle, triggering the investigation that led to the largest-ever biosecurity fine under a Queensland law.

gallery.altText: a woman in a fluro vest inspects cattle

thumbnail.altText: a woman in a fluro vest inspects cattle

items.title: Brazil authorities investigate two possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows

children.content: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

image.altText: A person wearing full-body and face PPE including a face mask standing in a medical tent.

items.title: Two new faces named as Socceroos reveal World Cup squad

children.content: Recently realigned Cristian Volpato and Japan-born Tete Yengi are in line for potential national debuts after being named in Australia's squad for this year's World Cup.

image.altText: Tony Popovic addresses the Socceroos squad during a training session

items.title: Australia scraps new Virginia-class submarines in AUKUS shake-up

children.content: The US, UK and Australia have announced they will "streamline" the purchase, with Australia no longer buying new submarines, and acquiring three second-hand ones.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: US Navy interested in Darwin ship lift, NT minister says

children.content: Northern Territory Infrastructure Minister Bill Yan says the United States Navy has expressed interest in using the controversial Darwin ship lift, which is expected to be operational next year.

image.altText: Construction site with several cranes on a harbour

items.title: Virgin holds $90m in unclaimed COVID flight credits. They expire in a month

children.content: The airline had more than $90 million in unspent travel credits on its books earlier this year.

gallery.altText: A Virgin plane is seen through an airport terminal glass

thumbnail.altText: A Virgin plane is seen through an airport terminal glass

items.title: Inhumane conditions at ICE detention camp, lawsuit says

children.content: Civil rights groups allege people at the largest immigration detention centre in Texas are being beaten, abused and denied medical care at the camp.

image.altText: Aerial view of ICE detention centre in the desert in Texas.

items.title: Officeworks plan to offshore white-collar jobs 'short-term solution'

children.content: The company says it wants a "globally connected team", but disgruntled staff say plans to offshore jobs is about replacing them with cheap labour.

image.altText: people walk past officeworks

items.title: Alarm as nursing cuts leave High Country town without after-hours care

children.content: Swifts Creek is an hour-and-a-half drive from the nearest hospital and residents are worried cuts to after-hours care at its Bush Nursing Centre might leave the community at risk.

gallery.altText: An older woman with a toddler and her mother in a nurse's waiting room

thumbnail.altText: An older woman with a toddler and her mother in a nurse's waiting room

items.title: Aussie Hindley on Giro podium as great Dane Vingegaard completes set

children.content: Australian Jai Hindley finishes third at the Giro d'Italia, while Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard adds a title in Rome to his victories in France and Spain.

image.altText: Jai Hindley shakes hands with Jonas Vingegaard as a firework goes off behind them after the Giro d'Italia.

items.title: West Coast claims fourth win as Essendon's injury nightmare continues

children.content: West Coast makes light work of Essendon at Perth Stadium, as the Bombers lose another promising youngster to a serious injury.

image.altText: Two AFL players in yellow and black celebrate a goal

items.title: Haas laments Broncos' BS as Maguire warns against hoping for another miracle

children.content: After four straight losses, the 5-7 Broncos are in the same position as last year before launching an audacious run to the premiership, but coach Michael Maguire says the team should not expect to be able to do that again.

image.altText: Payne Haas and the Brisbane Broncos stand behind their tryline.

items.title: Hundreds arrested as Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League win sparks riots

children.content: More than 50 police officers are wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalise shops, with one small group even trying to storm a Paris police station.

image.altText: Police are silhouetted as a firework explodes on a Paris street.

items.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut'

children.content: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.

gallery.altText: A crowed of people under purple and white jellyfish-like lanterns.

thumbnail.altText: A crowed of people under purple and white jellyfish-like lanterns.

items.title: Could native forests be part of Cement Australia's 'sustainable' fuel option?

children.content: Used tyres and wood waste will soon help to power a large cement manufacturing site in Tasmania as Cement Australia moves away from coal to alternative fuels, but some remain concerned the environmental benefit may be offset if the wood waste is sourced from logged native forests.

image.altText: Building Materias

items.title: 'Bit of a gold rush' as companies search for underground hydrogen

children.content: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

image.altText: A drill rig at night with trucks and lights surrounding it.

items.title: Dozens killed by blast in rebel-held Myanmar village

children.content: Authorities say the blast was likely caused by explosives used for mining that had been stored in a warehouse.

gallery.altText: The remnants of a house standing with debris scattered around it, in the distance a destroyed neighbourhood.

thumbnail.altText: The remnants of a house standing with debris scattered around it, in the distance a destroyed neighbourhood.
